world.title: Israel's continued strikes in Lebanon putting strain on fragile US-Iran ceasefire
world.description: Tehran warns it will respond it Israel doesn't immediately stop attacks on Lebanon, where at least 182 people have been killed.

world.title: WTI, Brent, Iran accuse U.S. of ceasefire breach
world.description: Oil markets rose after Iran accused the United States of violating elements of a two-week ceasefire agreement.

world.title: Vietnam and China launch AI-driven tourism cooperation programme to promote cross-border travel
world.description: Vietnam and China have launched a 14-day exchange and research programme in Nanning aimed at strengthening cooperation in cross-border tourism through the application of artificial intelligence, TV BRICS reports, citing VNA.

world.title: Trump criticises NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
world.description: US President Donald Trump vented his frustration with allies during a private meeting with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, as relations in the military alliance reached a crisis ⁠point over the Iran war.

business.description: Crisp skin? Check. Bold, glossy sauce? Check. In Tender, Lucy Tweed reimagines this Italian hunter’s stew as a spicy 60-minute flavour bomb.

business.title: Crude rises, stocks fall on fears over nascent Iran ceasefire
business.description: HONG KONG: Oil prices climbed and most stocks fell on Thursday (Apr 9) on fears over the nascent US-Iran ceasefire after Tehran threatened to resume hostilities as Israel launched a major bombardment of Lebanon.

business.title: Paramount Won't Need To Replace Jeff Shell, Insiders Say
business.description: Jeff Shell is unlikely to be directly replaced as president of Paramount, numerous insiders and people who do business with the company tell Deadline.

business.title: Why Carnival, Royal Caribbean, and Norwegian Cruise Line Stocks Surged Today
business.description: The potential for calmer seas has investors hopeful.

business.title: Nigeria Power Crisis Persists as Minister's Pledge Fails
business.description: Nigeria's power crisis continues two weeks after the Minister of Power promised improvements, with generation still low due to gas shortages and grid issue

business.title: China biotech flow picks up as Everest seals US$250 million deal
business.description: Sovereign wealth funds from Singapore and Abu Dhabi join Chinese state capital as cross-border biotech licensing reaches record highs.

business.title: China to further cut corporate burdens, dismantle market barriers in 2026
business.description: China will intensify efforts to reduce corporate burdens and dismantle institutional barriers this year to ensure the stability of industrial and supply chains and secure business operations, according to an inter-ministerial meeting held in Beijing on Wednesday.

science.title: ‘It really bent your mind’: The life-altering phenomenon astronauts experience in space
science.description: The astronauts' expressions of admiration and longing for Earth echo a long history of space explorers gaining new appreciation for their home planet.

science.title: Deadly heat stress conditions already occurring
science.description: Deadly heatwave events are occurring at temperatures previously thought to be survivable, Australian National University (ANU) research shows

science.title: New Simulations Reveal How Earth's Strongest Ocean Current Got Started
science.description: New simulations show that the world's strongest ocean current didn't start flowing overnight – several major factors needed to align before it could begin exerting its powerful influence on Earth's climate.

science.title: Prime Minister Carney joins CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen in a live call from deep space
science.description: Today, the Right Honourable Mark Carney, Prime Minister of Canada, spoke with Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Colonel Jeremy Hansen during a live space-to-Earth connection.

science.title: NASA's lunar success sharpens focus on China's 2030 crewed landing goal
science.description: Four U.S. astronauts on the Artemis II mission this week flew past the moon's shadowed far side, traveling deeper into space than any humans before.

science.title: Permian Fossil is Earliest Evidence of Rib-Powered Breathing
science.description: Paleontologists have examined 289-million-year-old specimens of the early reptile Captorhinus aguti that preserve a covering of three-dimensional skin, a complete shoulder girdle and ribcage with cartilages, and protein remnants that predate the previous oldest-known example by nearly 100 million years.

science.title: Fungus that rots trees from the inside may be hiding a climate issue
science.description: A common fungus that causes trees to rot may be exacerbating climate change by releasing more methane than expected.

ai.title: Kluisz rebrands as Nava, raises $21.7 million to build AI neocloud platform
ai.description: The startup is shifting from private cloud services to GPU-as-a-service infrastructure as investors bet on rising demand for AI computing capacity.

ai.title: AI drives Indian IT companies to cut US jobs
ai.description: Indian IT firms are increasing job cuts in the US, with layoffs expected to accelerate due to AI's growing influence and slowing deal-making. Companies are restructuring, impacting onsite employees, especially those tied to large transformation deals. Regulatory filings reveal a significant rise in layoffs in early 2026 compared to the previous year.

ai.title: backed IT companies growing faster, thanks to costs control, ops shifts, say experts
ai.description: Private equity backed IT firms are growing faster than public market companies. This is due to strong cost control and operational changes. These firms show higher risk appetite and focus on leadership. They are also quicker to adapt to AI-led disruption. This efficiency leads to higher market valuations. Investors are interested in their repeatable revenue and scalability.

ai.title: TR Capital to deploy $1 billion in India secondaries; appoints Umang Agarwal as MD
ai.description: TR Capital will deploy $1 billion in India over the next five years, across consumer, financial services, and healthcare, while selectively evaluating opportunities at the intersection of software and artificial intelligence (AI), managing partner Frederic Azemard told ET today.

ai.title: ETtech Explainer: Why Anthropic’s new AI model Mythos is a moment of reckoning
ai.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, can find thousands of critical security flaws, some decades old. Due to potential misuse, it is not being released publicly. Instead, about 40 companies, including tech giants, are testing it to fix bugs before attackers can exploit them. This development signals a major shift in cybersecurity.
